content.full_text:
FPPC

JUL 2 0 1992

July 16, 1992


Fair Political Practices Commission
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Subject: Tezak for Supervisor I.D.# 921087

Dear Gentlepersons:

The undersigned is (was)campaign manager for Wayne Tezak who was an
unsuccessful candidate for supervisor in Lassen County. The campaign
treasurer is (was) Wayne's wife, Helen Tezak.

When Wayne decided to run he made a decision not to accept any
campaign contributions and at no time has he, or his committee,
accepted any contributions whatsoever.

All funding for the campaign came directly from Wayne Tezak.

Wayne's wife, and treasurer, Helen Tezak, inadvertently entered the
money that Wayne advanced by way of loan on the contribution line
of the 490 form. the fact was that the amount entered as contribu-
tions was inadvertently entered improperly and was in fact a loan
by Wayne Tezak to his committee.

On the basis of these facts we ask that the F.P.P.C. consider the 490
amended on its face to reflect the fact the money was loaned and
therefore returnable to the lender, Wayne Tezak.

The facts alleged in this letter are true and correct and of my
personal knowledge, and could be provided by declaration under
penalty of perjury if so required.

Sincerely,

Robert E. Roney

RER/ht

sections.question_synthetic: The requester asks the FPPC to consider amending a Form 490 (campaign statement) for an unsuccessful supervisor candidate to reclassify improperly reported contributions as a loan from the candidate to his campaign committee.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC advice letter response. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present. Document contains request for administrative amendment with attestation of facts. Some OCR quality issues present ('inadverdently', 'lnlayne').

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: The requester asks the FPPC to consider amending a Form 490 (campaign statement) for an unsuccessful supervisor candidate to reclassify improperly reported contributions as a loan from the candidate to his campaign committee.

embedding.summary: Campaign manager Robert E. Roney requests that the FPPC amend Form 490 for Wayne Tezak's unsuccessful Lassen County supervisor campaign, requesting reclassification of funds that were incorrectly entered as contributions but were actually loans from the candidate to his campaign committee.
